title: Find the Duplicate Number
problem_statement: Given an array nums containing n + 1 integers where each integer is between 1 and n inclusive, return the duplicate number without modifying the array and using only constant extra space.
intuition: Treat the array as a linked list of indices. A duplicate creates a cycle, and cycle detection finds the repeated value.

approaches.name: Floyd’s Cycle Detection
approaches.description: Use tortoise and hare pointers on the implicit linked list defined by values → indices to locate the cycle start.

interview_qa.q: Why does this problem have a cycle?
interview_qa.a: The range 1..n and n+1 numbers force at least one value to repeat, which makes the implicit next-pointer graph cyclic.

interview_qa.q: What if you sort the array?
interview_qa.a: Sorting would find the duplicate but requires modifying the input and costs O(n log n). The cycle method is O(n) and space O(1).
